questions.q: What is constant folding in compiler optimization?
options: Evaluating constant expressions at compile time and replacing them with their computed result
Removing unused variables from a program
Rearranging loop iterations for better cache performance
Combining multiple variable declarations into a single statement
questions.explanation: Constant folding replaces compile-time computable expressions like 3 + 4 with 7. Removing unused variables is dead code elimination; loop rearrangement is a different optimization; declaration combining is a syntax transformation.

options: Removing code that is unreachable or whose results are never used
Deleting comments from source code before compilation
Eliminating branches with missing else clauses
Converting recursive functions into iterative ones
questions.explanation: Dead code elimination removes unreachable or useless code without changing program semantics. Removing comments is a lexical operation; missing else clauses are syntactically valid; recursion-to-iteration is a different transformation.

questions.q: What is loop unrolling and why might it be beneficial?
options: Replicating loop body statements to reduce branch overhead and improve pipeline efficiency
Converting a loop into recursive function calls for better readability
Eliminating all loops from a program to prevent infinite execution
Inverting loop conditions to reverse iteration direction
questions.explanation: Loop unrolling reduces branching and enables vectorization. Converting to recursion reduces performance; eliminating loops changes semantics; inverting conditions changes behavior.

questions.q: What is Static Single Assignment (SSA) form?
options: An intermediate representation where each variable is assigned exactly once
A requirement that all variables must be statically typed
A constraint that functions can have only one return statement
A property of assembly code where registers are assigned one per instruction
questions.explanation: In SSA, each variable receives exactly one assignment, making data flow analysis easier. Static typing is a language property, not SSA; single returns are a coding style; register assignment is not what SSA means.

questions.q: Why is SSA form useful for compiler optimization?
options: It makes data flow analysis straightforward because each variable's value comes from exactly one source
It reduces the number of registers required on the target machine
It eliminates the need for loop optimization
It allows direct execution of intermediate code without machine code generation
questions.explanation: SSA simplifies tracking where values come from, enabling many analyses. It doesn't reduce register count (that's register allocation); doesn't eliminate loop optimization; doesn't make intermediate code executable directly.

options: Identifying identical expressions in code and computing them once, reusing the result
Removing redundant conditional statements
Merging multiple functions that compute the same result
Eliminating operator precedence issues in arithmetic expressions
questions.explanation: CSE avoids redundant computation by computing a subexpression once and reusing it. Redundant conditionals are dead code; merging functions changes program structure; precedence issues are syntactic, not optimization-related.

questions.q: What is the purpose of live variable analysis in optimization?
options: Determining which variables contain values that might be used in the future
Tracking variable names for the debugger's symbol table
Identifying variables that are explicitly initialized in their declarations
Counting the frequency of variable accesses in a program
questions.explanation: Live variable analysis identifies values needed later, guiding decisions like register allocation and dead code removal. It's not about debug symbols, initialization patterns, or access frequency.

questions.q: How can peephole optimization improve code?
options: By examining and replacing small sequences of instructions with more efficient equivalents
By analyzing global data flow across entire functions
By predicting runtime behavior without executing code
By eliminating all function call overhead through inlining
questions.explanation: Peephole optimization works on short local instruction sequences, finding inefficiencies. Global data flow analysis is broader; runtime prediction isn't peephole optimization; inlining is a separate technique.

questions.q: What is the difference between inlining and loop unrolling?
options: Inlining replaces function calls with function body; unrolling replicates loop iterations
Inlining applies only to recursive functions; unrolling applies only to nested loops
Unrolling works at runtime; inlining happens only at compile time
Inlining requires explicit annotations; unrolling is automatic in all compilers
questions.explanation: Inlining substitutes a function call with its body; unrolling repeats loop bodies. Both apply broadly; both happen at compile time; neither requires special annotations universally.

questions.q: What does a correctness-preserving optimization guarantee?
options: The optimized program produces the same observable results as the original for all valid inputs
The optimized program uses less memory than the original
The optimized program always runs faster than the original
The optimized program is easier to understand and maintain
questions.explanation: Correctness preservation means semantics are unchanged. Optimizations may not always reduce memory or speed; readability and maintainability are secondary concerns.
